name: HMS Pearl (T 22)
events: 15 Apr 1941 HMS Kipling (Cdr. A. St. Clair-Ford, RN) departed Milford Haven to escort a 15 ship coastal convoy from the Bristol Channel to Falmouth. Also part of the escort were the armed yacht HMS Viva II (Cdr.(Retd.) M.A. Blomfield, OBE, RN) and the A/S trawlers HMS Pearl (T/Lt. G. Pemberton, RNR) and HMS Ruby (T/Lt. N.L. Brown, RNVR) (1)

16 Apr 1941 The coastal convoy HMS Kipling (Cdr. A. St. Clair-Ford, RN) is escorting together with the armed yacht HMS Viva II (Cdr.(Retd.) M.A. Blomfield, OBE, RN) and the A/S trawlers HMS Pearl (T/Lt. G. Pemberton, RNR) and HMS Ruby (T/Lt. N.L. Brown, RNVR) is attacked north of Lands End in position 50°27'N, 05°38'W by enemy aircraft between 0228 and 0345 hours. The of the merchant vessels were sunk; Amiens (British, 1548 GRT, built 1918), Anglesea Rose (British, 1151 GRT, built 1930) and Bolette (Norwegian, 1167 GRT, built 1920). (1)

1 Dec 1941 HMS P 36 (Lt. H.N. Edmonds, DSC, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Dartmouth with HMS Cleveland (Cdr. G.B. Sayer, RN), HMS Coriolanus (T/S.Lt. I.R. Saddler, RNVR), HMS Pearl (T/Lt. G.K. Hill, RNVR) and HMS Lord Essenden (Skr. J.T. Denoon, RNR).
HMS P 36 also carried out gunnery exercises on a target that was being towed by HMS Mangrove (T/Lt. J.K.M. Warde, RNVR) (1)

1 Apr 1943 Around 0930 A/1, HMS Ultimatum (Lt. W.H. Kett, RNR) departed Plymouth for Holy Loch. She was to rendezvous around 1400 A/1 with HMS La Capricieuse (Lt.Cdr. G.W. Dobson, RNR) off the Scilly Islands for onward passage north through the Irish Sea.
was escorted from Plymouth to the rendezvous by HMS Pearl (T/Lt. G.K. Hill, RNVR). (2)

8 Apr 1943 HMS Taku (Lt. A.J.W. Pitt, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with HMS Pearl (T/Lt. G.K. Hill, RNVR) and HMS Ulva (Skr. E.R. Harris, RNR) and later with HMS Ambrose Pare (Skr. J.W. Morris, RNR) and HMS Lincolnshire (A/Skr.Lt. S.L. Larner, RNR). Also a practice attack was made on HMS Lincolnshire. (3)

28 Apr 1944 HMS Seraph (Lt. T. Russell-Walling, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with HMS Pearl (T/Lt. B.G. Barfoot, RNVR), HMS Gweal (T/Lt. P.E.C.R. Main, RNVR), HMS La Nantaise (Skr. S.G. Cory, RNR) and HMS Lord Wakefield (Skr.Lt. H.E. Dodd, RNR). (4)

23 Dec 1944 HMS Pearl (T/Lt. B.G. Barfoot, RNVR) picks up 51 survivors from the British merchant Dumfries that is torpedoed and sunk by German U-boat U-322 south of St.Catherine?s Point, Isle of Wight in position 50°23'N, 01°43'W.
23 Apr 1945 Around 0900 B/23, HMS Unrivalled (Lt. R.P. Fitzgerald, DSC, RN) departed Plymouth for exercises with aircraft to the north of Brittany, France.
At 1640 B/23, HMS Unrivalled arrived in her exercises area. Her escort from Plymouth to the exercise area, HMS Pearl (T/Lt. B.G. Barfoot, RNVR), then parted company. (5)
30 Apr 1945 Around 0915 B/30, HMS Unrivalled (Lt. R.P. Fitzgerald, DSC, RN) departed Plymouth for exercises with aircraft to the north of Brittany, France.
At 1645 B/23, HMS Unrivalled arrived in her exercises area. Her escort from Plymouth to the exercise area, HMS Pearl (T/Lt. B.G. Barfoot, RNVR), then parted company. (5)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
